title: Product Catalog Plans Prices Semantic Contract
description: Coverage-complete semantic contract for the product catalog plans prices capability. Semantic information captured as a USF coverage record via the coverage record and equivalence audit; no external source code is imported and the V2 implementation language is an open decision.
aiGuidance: Coverage-complete draft grounded in semantic information (USF coverage record only; no source import; language open). Proof is hermetic/stale lineage, not current readiness. Enrich detail and publish fresh proof before generation; respect the hexagonal constraint in ADR 0005.

lifecycle.description: The catalog lifecycle creates and lists products, then plans optionally filtered by product, then prices optionally filtered by plan, while tenant reads return the combined active catalog view. All write paths are operator-scoped and the in-house catalog is treated as the semantic source of truth for billable products, plans, and prices. Catalog mutations carry actor identity into the use-case layer so changes are attributable.

stateModel.description: State is a three-level hierarchy: Product (identifier, name, description, active flag, created timestamp), Plan (identifier, owning product, name, currency, billing period, active flag, created timestamp), and Price (identifier, owning plan, version, price type, unit amount, currency, billing period, active flag, created timestamp). Items carry explicit active/inactive state rather than being hard-deleted, and price versioning preserves historical amounts. State changes flow through ordered migrations that must preserve this proven model.

permissions.description: Operator catalog list and create operations require platform billing administration permission with read/write authorization; tenant catalog reads require a tenant billing-read permission and are scoped to the requesting organisation. Permission and entitlement checks run before any adapter call, and tenant context cannot read or mutate another tenant's catalog view.

contracts.description: The contract surface exposes operator create/list operations for products, plans, and prices plus a combined tenant catalog read, with plan and price list operations accepting product/plan filter parameters. Create payloads conform to per-entity request schemas, and the interface is mediated by a catalog port with a relational adapter so callers never bind to storage directly.

validation.description: Create bodies are validated against product, plan, and price request schemas before any persistence call, rejecting malformed input with a typed validation outcome up front. Currency, billing-period, price-type, and amount fields are explicitly typed, and references between price, plan, and product must resolve.

errorModel.description: Invalid create bodies fail closed with a validation error and permission denials are returned before handlers execute, so no partial catalog write occurs. Successful creates return the created entity with a creation status while list operations return the current set; provider failure degrades to a typed readiness failure rather than fabricating catalog state.

auditModel.description: Catalog mutations record a catalog-changed audit action carrying the acting operator identity into the use-case and adapter layers, so every product/plan/price change is attributable. Route-registration proof is explicitly not treated as an audit proof; audit is a distinct, mutation-bound record.

readinessModel.description: Readiness is migration- and catalog-seed-backed: route-registration evidence validates the exposed surface and unit behaviour validates in-process catalog operations, while live provider billing readiness is tracked as a separate capability. When the catalog adapter is unavailable, mutation paths are disabled and only read-only readiness and audit remain visible.

proof.description: Behaviour is exercised by catalog unit tests and a catalog route-registration runtime proof, with live HTTP-backed plan synchronisation validated separately through the external billing provider adapter. Proof is hermetic/runtime lineage; fresh USF proof pending USF-101/USF-59.

uiSemanticDefinition.description: No distinct UI capability at the contract level (coverage matrix ui=0); UI is modelled separately and remains a future AI-customised deliverable.
